title:
Starting from the existing OpenSensorHub MAVSDK addon at https://github.com/opensensorhub/osh-addons/tree/master/sensors/robotics/sensorhub-driver-mavsdk, design and implement MAVLink/MAVSDK support for OpenSensorHub through the OGC Connected Systems API.

Treat the upstream addon as the baseline, not a clean-room rewrite. Preserve its OSH sensor module patterns, MAVSDK Java integration, mavsdk_server lifecycle, existing telemetry outputs, and existing control inputs unless the architecture explicitly replaces them.

The implementation must provide full Connected Systems API coverage for MAVSDK plugins. For every plugin exposed by the pinned MAVSDK Java/proto version, produce a coverage matrix mapping the plugin's methods and streams to one of:
- CS API DataStream + Observation
- CS API ControlStream + Command + CommandStatus/CommandResult
- SystemEvent
- explicit unsupported/deferred entry with rationale

Prefer typed MAVSDK plugin integrations for semantic APIs. Also evaluate MAVLink-native access and implement a generic MAVLink fallback using MavlinkDirect or a native MAVLink library where needed for raw messages, custom dialects, or plugin gaps. Do not hand-roll MAVLink framing, do not stub MAVSDK/OSH classes, and do not claim full coverage without a machine-checkable coverage inventory.

Acceptance:
1. The driver starts a real mavsdk_server and connects to a real or simulated MAVLink system.
2. CS API exposes typed datastreams for telemetry/status/info/events and typed controlstreams for actions, missions, offboard/manual control, params, camera/gimbal, geofence, FTP/logs, calibration, RTK, shell/tune, transponder/winch/gripper, server-side plugins where applicable.
3. A generic raw MAVLink datastream/controlstream supports subscribe-all, subscribe-by-message-name, send-message, and load-custom-XML dialect.
4. Long-running commands expose status/result resources, not just fire-and-forget acknowledgements.
5. Tests include schema/coverage tests plus at least one live MAVSDK/SITL smoke test.
6. README documents MAVSDK vs native-MAVLink tradeoffs and the coverage matrix.

Test run: 1780368353973
review_summary: Plan is approved. It meets all completeness criteria and has no structural defects.
last_error: autonomous mode: 4/5 requirements failed; auto-rejecting (production mode would await human decision)
goal: Design and implement MAVLink/MAVSDK support for OpenSensorHub via the OGC Connected Systems API, incorporating typed streams, a raw MAVLink fallback, long-running command support, and a complete coverage matrix with SITL testing.
context: OpenSensorHub requires a fully compliant OGC Connected Systems API driver for MAVLink-based unmanned systems. The current upstream implementation provides a baseline but needs comprehensive mapping of MAVSDK plugins to CS API paradigms (DataStreams, ControlStreams, Observation, Command) as well as raw MAVLink fallback and reliable lifecycle management for mavsdk_server. A machine-checkable coverage matrix is necessary to ensure semantic APIs are systematically mapped or explicitly deferred.

capabilities.name: mavsdk-server-lifecycle
capabilities.description: Manage the embedded mavsdk_server binary process, establish the MAVLink system connection, and maintain the MAVSDK Java client instance lifecycle.

capabilities.name: telemetry-datastream
capabilities.description: Expose MAVSDK telemetry, info, and event plugins as OGC Connected Systems API DataStreams and Observations.

capabilities.name: control-datastream
capabilities.description: Bridge MAVSDK action, mission, and component control plugins to OGC Connected Systems API ControlStreams.

capabilities.name: async-command-status
capabilities.description: Track asynchronous, long-running MAVSDK commands and expose their progress via CS API CommandStatus and CommandResult resources.

capabilities.name: raw-mavlink-fallback
capabilities.description: Provide a generic MAVLink fallback mechanism for raw message pub/sub and custom dialects via CS API streams, bypassing typed MAVSDK plugins when necessary.

open_questions: Which MAVSDK Java/proto version should the implementation pin against?
Should the raw MAVLink fallback use MAVSDK's MavlinkPassthrough plugin, or rely on an entirely separate native MAVLink library connection (e.g., MavlinkDirect)?
How should the SITL environment be provisioned for the live smoke tests?

technology_choices.rationale: Project explicitly sets sourceCompatibility = JavaVersion.VERSION_17 in build.gradle.
Provides typed Java bindings for MAVLink over gRPC to a mavsdk_server instance. Required by prompt.
Target platform for the OGC Connected Systems API driver.

component_boundaries.name: mavsdk-osh-driver
component_boundaries.responsibility: Provides MAVLink/MAVSDK support to OpenSensorHub. Maps MAVSDK plugins to OGC CS API paradigms and manages the mavsdk_server lifecycle.

architecture.data_flow: OGC CS Client <-> OGC ConSys API <-> MAVSDK OSH Driver <-> MAVSDK Java Client <-> gRPC <-> mavsdk_server <-> MAVLink UDP <-> PX4 SITL Autopilot

decisions.title: Manage mavsdk_server as a subprocess
decisions.decision: The driver will spawn and manage the native mavsdk_server executable as a child process.
decisions.rationale: MAVSDK Java does not bundle a desktop-compatible embedded server (it provides an 'aar' for Android), so the desktop/server driver must manage the binary lifecycle directly. (Ref: https://github.com/mavlink/MAVSDK-Java/blob/main/README.md)

decisions.title: Extend AbstractSensorModule
decisions.rationale: Standard OSH driver pattern for integrating external systems. (Ref: https://github.com/opensensorhub/osh-core/blob/master/sensorhub-core/src/main/java/org/sensorhub/impl/sensor/AbstractSensorModule.java)

integration_flows.name: sitl-telemetry-and-control
integration_flows.description: Connects to PX4 SITL, receives MAVLink telemetry, maps to OGC DataStreams, and issues a command that succeeds.

steps: Client subscribes to OGC DataStream for telemetry
Driver receives MAVLink telemetry and pushes to DataStream
Driver maps to MAVSDK action and executes
success_criteria: Telemetry is received via OGC CS API

apis.signature: public System(@NonNull String host, int port)
apis.lifecycle: new System(host, port) -> telemetry() / action() -> dispose()
apis.notes: Main entry point for MAVSDK Java client, initializes plugins lazily.

apis.signature: public abstract class AbstractSensorModule<TConfig extends SensorConfig>

apis.notes: Used as readiness and telemetry proof for the driver.

harness_profiles.purpose: Prove MAVSDK control and telemetry paths against a real PX4 SITL MAVLink endpoint.

requirements.title: MAVSDK server and client lifecycle management
requirements.description: The system MUST manage the embedded mavsdk_server binary process, establishing and maintaining the connection to a MAVLink system. The driver SHALL start the server, connect a MAVSDK Java client instance, and successfully pass a live MAVSDK/SITL smoke test.
requirements.recovery_hint:
Recommended action: refine_prompt
Recovery agent confidence: recovery_succeeded=true
Original wedge: validation failures exceeded TDD cycle budget
Recovery agent trajectory: b21e4321-b27d-42ea-b321-eebd46dbc26f
Wedged agent trajectory: ff1985cf-fc05-4ce5-8be9-8f5f371efce1

Diagnosis:
The agent is stuck between a logic failure in a lifecycle test and a structural validation failure regarding harness environment variables. The trajectory shows the agent modifying `MavsdkServerLifecycleTest.java` but failing to address the `harness-profile-discipline` requirement, which expects tests to consume environment variables like `PX4_SIM_MODEL` instead of using hardcoded defaults. The agent is thrashed by the `unitRestartIfExitedExposesReconnectingStatusBeforeReplacementLaunch` failure, likely because it is not correctly simulating the 'exited' state or the subsequent 'reconnecting' transition in its test setup.

Excerpts:
- 'MavsdkServerLifecycleTest > unitRestartIfExitedExposesReconnectingStatusBeforeReplacementLaunch() FAILED'
- 'no modified test file references any of the profile's declared env keys ("PX4_SIM_MODEL") — the test appears to be reading hardcoded endpoints'

requirements.title: Telemetry DataStreams via OGC CS API
requirements.description: The system MUST expose MAVSDK telemetry, information, and event plugins as OGC Connected Systems API DataStreams and Observations. A machine-checkable coverage matrix MUST be provided that maps each pinned plugin's streams to CS API paradigms or explicitly justifies its deferral.

requirements.title: Control ControlStreams via OGC CS API
requirements.description: The system MUST bridge MAVSDK action, mission, and component control plugins to OGC Connected Systems API ControlStreams. Like telemetry, these typed streams MUST be documented in the coverage matrix to demonstrate systematic integration of the semantic API.

requirements.title: Asynchronous command status and results
requirements.description: The system SHALL track asynchronous, long-running MAVSDK commands instead of treating them as fire-and-forget. The progress and completion states MUST be exposed via CS API CommandStatus and CommandResult resources.

requirements.title: Raw MAVLink fallback streams
requirements.description: The system MUST provide a generic MAVLink fallback mechanism for raw message publish/subscribe and custom XML dialect loading through CS API streams. The README MUST document the tradeoffs between this native MAVLink approach and the typed MAVSDK integrations.

scenarios.title: ControlMapper updates CommandStatus for successful MAVSDK action
scenarios.given: a ControlMapper instance and a mock CommandStatus tracker
scenarios.when: a long-running command (e.g., Takeoff) is initiated and the internal MAVSDK observer emits a success event
then: the CommandStatus status transitions to 'ACCEPTED'
the CommandStatus status eventually transitions to 'COMPLETED' upon receipt of the success callback

scenarios.title: ControlMapper reports failure in CommandStatus when MAVSDK action fails
scenarios.given: a ControlMapper instance and a mock CommandStatus tracker
scenarios.when: a command is initiated and the internal MAVSDK observer emits a 'Command Denied' error event
then: the CommandStatus status transitions to 'FAILED'
the CommandResult contains the error message 'Action denied by vehicle'

scenarios.title: Asynchronous takeoff command emits progress and completion to OGC CS API
scenarios.given: the SITL endpoint at env $SITL_ENDPOINT, and the MAVSDK driver active and connected to the drone
scenarios.when: an OGC CS Client sends a ControlStream request for 'Takeoff' to the driver endpoint
then: the CS API returns a CommandStatus resource for the 'Takeoff' request with status 'IN_PROGRESS'
the CommandStatus status transitions to 'COMPLETED' after the drone reaches takeoff altitude

scenarios.title: mavsdk_server process command line generation
scenarios.given: a configuration object with a valid local path to a mavsdk_server binary and a valid connection URL
scenarios.when: the server process manager prepares the startup command
then: the command line arguments include the specified connection URL
the process builder is initialized with the correct executable path

scenarios.title: driver attempts reconnection when mavsdk_server process terminates unexpectedly
scenarios.given: a MAVSDK driver instance configured to retry connection on failure
scenarios.when: the mavsdk_server process exits with a non-zero code
then: the driver status indicates it is attempting to reconnect
the process manager attempts to restart the mavsdk_server process

scenarios.title: successful connection to PX4 SITL via mavsdk_server
scenarios.given: the PX4 SITL endpoint at environment variable $SITL_UDP_ENDPOINT
scenarios.when: the MAVSDK driver is initialized and started with the SITL endpoint
then: the MAVSDK Java client reports a successful connection to the system
the driver status transitions to 'CONNECTED' within 30 seconds
a MAVLink HEARTBEAT message is detected through the MAVSDK connection

scenarios.title: TelemetryMapper converts MAVSDK Position to OGC Observation Point
scenarios.given: a TelemetryMapper instance and a MAVSDK Position object with latitude 47.397, longitude 8.545, and absolute altitude 488.0
scenarios.when: the mapper is called to transform the position telemetry
then: the mapper returns an OGC Observation containing a Point geometry with coordinates [8.545, 47.397, 488.0]
the Observation result includes the correct UOM for altitude as 'm'

scenarios.title: Position DataStream emits observations from PX4 SITL via CS API
scenarios.given: the SITL endpoint at env $SITL_ENDPOINT and the mavsdk-osh-driver configured to connect to it
scenarios.when: an OGC CS Client subscribes to the position DataStream and the vehicle is armed and hovering
then: the OGC CS API endpoint /datastreams/position/observations returns a valid observation within 15 seconds
the observation data matches the current SITL vehicle coordinates

scenarios.title: TelemetryMapper handles unmapped MAVSDK plugin data gracefully
scenarios.given: a TelemetryMapper initialized with an empty mapping configuration
scenarios.when: an unsupported MAVSDK telemetry event is passed to the mapper
then: the mapper returns an empty optional or throws a DataMappingException
no malformed OGC Observation is produced

scenarios.title: Custom XML dialect loading is passed to the server process
scenarios.given: a MAVSDK driver configuration with a custom XML dialect path provided
scenarios.when: the driver prepares the server process command line args
then: the driver initializes the MAVSDK server process with the --custom-xml-path argument matching the provided path
the dialect loader confirms the custom message types are registered in the internal message dictionary

scenarios.title: Raw MAVLink message subscription via CS API stream against SITL
scenarios.given: the OGC CS API stream configured for raw MAVLink data with a specific message ID filter
scenarios.when: a raw MAVLink message is broadcast by the SITL autopilot on the monitored UDP port
then: the CS API stream receives the raw MAVLink packets for the requested message ID within 5 seconds
the raw payload matches the bitstream expected from the SITL simulated telemetry

scenarios.title: Raw MAVLink message publishing to SITL via CS API ControlStream
scenarios.given: the MAVSDK driver is connected to the SITL endpoint via the test harness environment variables
scenarios.when: a raw MAVLink packet is sent through the OGC CS ControlStream endpoint
then: the SITL autopilot receives the raw MAVLink packet and acknowledges receipt if applicable
the MAVSDK driver logs the successful dispatch of the raw message to the autopilot link

scenarios.title: Graceful fallback when custom dialect path is invalid or missing
scenarios.given: a configuration object with a missing dialect path but raw fallback enabled
then: the driver successfully initializes using the standard common.xml dialect
the driver logs a warning that custom message decoding will be limited to the standard dialect

scenarios.title: ControlMapper maps Takeoff command to MAVSDK Action plugin
scenarios.given: a ControlMapper initialized with a mock MAVSDK Action plugin and a target CS API ControlStream definition for 'Takeoff'
scenarios.when: the mapping function is invoked for the Takeoff action
then: the mapper produces a CS API Command structure with double-typed 'altitude' parameter
the mapper's execution function invokes the MAVSDK Action 'takeoff' method when the command is received

scenarios.title: Issue Takeoff command via OGC CS ControlStream against SITL
scenarios.given: the PX4 SITL and MAVSDK Server started by the test harness at $MAVSDK_SERVER_ADDRESS
scenarios.when: a POST request containing a Takeoff command is sent to the OGC CS ControlStream endpoint
then: the OGC CS API endpoint returns a 202 Accepted response
the MAVSDK Action plugin reports the drone has transitioned to 'Taking Off' state
the drone altitude in SITL increases above 1 meter within 30 seconds

scenarios.title: ControlMapper handles invalid mission parameters gracefully
scenarios.given: a ControlMapper configured for MAVSDK Mission uploads with a valid CS API Mission schema
scenarios.when: a Mission command is received with an empty waypoint array
then: the mapper rejects the command with a 'Missing Parameter' error for the waypoint list

scenarios.title: Failed ControlStream command returns MAVSDK error details
scenarios.given: the PX4 SITL environment at $MAVSDK_SERVER_ADDRESS with the drone currently in 'Hold' mode
scenarios.when: a 'Land' command is issued via OGC CS API while the drone is already on the ground
then: the OGC CS API returns a 500 Internal Server Error or appropriate error JSON
the error message indicates 'No landable surface' or 'Command denied' from MAVSDK

plan_decisions.title: Recovery for requirement.3e7e6046bbe8.1: refine_prompt
plan_decisions.rationale:
Recommended action: refine_prompt
Recovery agent confidence: recovery_succeeded=true
Original wedge: validation failures exceeded TDD cycle budget
Recovery agent trajectory: 6d67fc6e-fe54-4ece-8dc0-0e039d2259bc
Wedged agent trajectory: 0bde99bc-9665-4134-96d7-0b719b026f83

Diagnosis:
The trajectory shows the agent stuck in a TDD cycle where tests fail with `java.lang.ClassNotFoundException`. The build output explicitly reports `Task :compileJava NO-SOURCE`, yet the agent only modified the test file. The agent failed to recognize that it must create the implementation class in `src/main/java` to resolve the dependency issues, as evidenced by 'Task :compileJava NO-SOURCE' and the missing implementation files in the directory listing.
